revision.text:
[[ja:藤島神社 (諏訪地方)]] {{Nihongo|'''Fujishima Shrine'''|ふじしまじんじゃ}} refers to two [[Shrines in Japan|Shinto shrines]] located in the [[Suwa Region|Suwa region]] of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. Both shrines are deeply connected to the regional tradition of [[Takeminakata]], the deity enshrined at [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Grand Shrine]]. Due to their shared origins, they are discussed together in this article. == Fujishima Shrine (Suwa City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Fujishima Shrine |image =File:Fujishima Shrine (Suwa) - 藤島社（諏訪市）.jpg |location = {{JAW|Nagano Prefecture|Nagano}} {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|qid=Q846338|lt=Suwa City|1=Suwa, Nagano}} Nakasu (typeer [[Nakasu Village (Nagano)|Nakasu Village]]) Jingū-ji |coordinates = {{Coord|36.0025|N|138.11888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto]]
[[Takeminakata|Fujishima Myōjin]] (possibly) |Rank = [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Grand Shrine]] Kamisha auxiliary shrine ([[Suwa Taisha#Auxiliary and subordinate shrines|"Mi11le Thirteen Shrines"]] primary shrine) |founded = Unknown |alternate_name = Fujishima Daimyōjin, Fuji Suwamori |rituals = Rice Planting Festival (1st Sunday in June) |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}} }}This shrine serves as an [[auxiliary shrine]] of the [[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]] Upper Shrine (Kamisha), specifically the lead shrine of the "[[Suwa Taisha#Auxiliary and subordinate shrines|Mi11le Thirteen Shrines]]." It is located approximately 500 meters north of Kamisha’s Honmiya (Main Shrine). According to local myths in the Suwa region, [[Takeminakata]] entered Suwa and fought against the [[Kunitsukami]] deity [[Moriya|Moreya]]. In the legend, Takeminakata subdued Moreya, who wielded iron weapons, using a [[wisteria]] branch. Takeminakata then planted or discarded the branch, which flourished into a vibrant wisteria tree. This story is recorded in documents such as the {{ill|1249|en|1249|qid=Q5485|lt=1249|1=1249}} "[[Suwa Shinju Kaisetsu|Letter of Suwa Nobushige]]" and the {{ill|1356|en|1356|qid=Q6130|lt=1356|1=1356}} "{{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|qid=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|1=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}}."Suwa City History Editorial Committee, "Chapter 8, The Suwa Nobushige Letter and the Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba," in ''Suwa City History, Volume 1: Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval,'' 1995, pp. 811–814. In the "Ekotoba," this tradition is explicitly linked to the founding of Fujishima Shrine.Suwa City History Editorial Committee, "Section 2, Suwa Shrine Kamisha and Shimosha," in ''Suwa City History, Volume 1: Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval,'' 1995, pp. 682–683. {{quotation|The deity of Fujishima Myōjin, in ancient times, subdued the bandit Moreya when he disrupted divine order. Wielding iron rings, Moreya fought against the god, who used wisteria branches to bring him to submission. The god cast down the branch, which took root and flourished, marking the battlefield for eternity. Thus, the shrine is known as Fujishima Myōjin."[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/936498/45 Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba]," in ''Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Volume 73,'' 1925, pp. 494–539.}} In recent years, the shrine was relocated to its current site southeast of the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|qid=Q1091276|lt=Chūō Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}} due to local redevelopment projects.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji,'' Kyōdo Shuppansha, 1992, pp. 88–93. === Deities === * [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto]] * Fujishima Myōjin (Takeminakata?) === Rituals === * '''Rice Planting Festival''' (early June) :Adjacent to the shrine is a sacred rice field ("[[御作田]]"), where Suwa Grand Shrine’s Rice Planting Festival is held annually. The planted rice ripens within a month and is offered at the shrine, a phenomenon known as the "early-ripening rice of the sacred field," one of the [[Suwa Taisha#Seven Mysteries|Seven Mysteries of Suwa Taisha]]. :In medieval times, the three days preceding the Rice Planting Festival included the "Sacred Field Hunt," where hunted game was offered to the shrine. Following the festival, a purification ritual involving the release of [[Shinto rituals#Shimenawa|great offerings]] into the river took place. == Fujishima Shrine (Okaya City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Fujishima Shrine |image =Fujishima Shrine - 藤島神社 (川岸天竜河畔諏訪明神入諏伝説の地).jpg |location = {{JAW|Nagano Prefecture|Nagano}} {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|qid=Q838672|lt=Okaya City|1=Okaya, Nagano}} Kawagishi Kami 1-1 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|11|N|138|02|03|E|region:JP}} |deity = [[Takeminakata|Fujishima Myōjin]]
[[:en:Sanbō Kōjin|Sanbō Kōjin]] |Rank = |founded = Unknown |alternate_name = Fujishima Daimyōjin, Ryō Myōjin, Jūjūsho Myōjin, Arakami-zuka |rituals = festival (2nd Sunday in March) }} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Arakami-zuka Kofun |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound]] (now destroyed) |size = Width: 1.8m; Length: 7.2m (recorded) |burial = |burial goods = [[Magatama]], [[Kudatama|Tubular beads]], [[:en:bridles|bridles]], [[straight swords]], {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} fragments (bottles, jars), etc. |period = Late 6th–early 7th century |identity = Unknown |landmark status = }} This shrine is located in Kawagishi Kami, Okaya City, along [[Nagano Prefecture Route 14]]. It occupies the site of the [[Arakami-zuka Kofun]]. [[File:Fujishima_Shrine_(藤島社).png|thumb|the shrine in 1937]] This Kofun is unique among Suwa’s ancient burial sites for its flat location near the [[Tenryū River]]. Constructed between the late 6th and early 7th centuries, it likely marks the grave of a warrior or administrator who controlled this strategic location. Over time, it became associated with Fujishima Shrine and the local deity [[:en:Sanbō Kōjin|Sanbō Kōjin]].Mizawa Ward History Editing Committee, ''History of Mizawa Ward,'' 2013, p. 16. According to tradition, the shrine’s sacred [[wisteria]] once extended across the river to the opposite bank, where it intertwined with the sacred tree of [[Moriya Shrine|Moreya Shrine]]. A woodcutter who cut the wisteria on orders from the {{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|qid=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain|1=Suwa Domain}} lord was said to have gone mad and died shortly afterward.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji,'' Kyōdo Shuppansha, 1992, pp. 88–93. === Deities === * Fujishima Myōjin (Takeminakata) * [[:en:Sanbō Kōjin|Sanbō Kōjin]] (Night Gods, Wrathful Kōjin, Mishima Kōjin)Mizawa Ward History Editing Committee, ''History of Mizawa Ward,'' 2013, p. 308. === Rituals === * '''festival''' (2nd Sunday in March) :Conducted by the Shrine Committee. === Access === * {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} {{ill|Okaya Station|en|Okaya Station|qid=Q843826|lt=Okaya Station|1=Okaya Station}}—15 minutes on foot (approx. 1.3 km) == References == {{reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Imai Nogiku|year=1976|title=The Land of Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankō Kai|id=BB07630391}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor= ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] [[Template:infobox ancient site]] [[File:Fujishima Shrine (Nakasu, Suwa) - 藤島社（諏訪市中洲）- 2.jpg|280px]] [[File:Fujishima Shrine - 藤島神社 (川岸天竜河畔諏訪明神入諏伝説の地).jpg |280px]] {{translated page|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)|version=98270189|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:藤島神社 (諏訪地方)]] {{wikidata link|Q56883936}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Okaya]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa]] [[Category:History of Okaya]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
